---
title: Last Updated at Top
description: How Trellis moves the last-updated date and edit links to the top of each page.
keywords: [last-updated, edit, theme, docitem]
last_update:
  date: 02/20/2026
  author: Trellis
---

In many documentation frameworks, the last-updated date and "Edit this page" link appear at the **bottom** of the page, in the footer area. Trellis moves them to the **top**, directly below the page title.

## Why Top Placement?

Readers want to know immediately whether the content they're reading is current. Placing the date at the top gives instant context about freshness — no scrolling required. This is especially important for documentation that changes frequently, like API references or configuration guides.

## How It Works

The customization lives in `components/docs/DocItem/Content/index.js`. The key changes:

1. **`DocItemHeader` component** — renders a `LastUpdatedRow` with the date, "Edit this page" link, and "Suggest an Edit" link
2. **Placement** — `DocItemHeader` is rendered inside the `<header>` block, right after the page title heading
3. **"Suggest an Edit" link** — a Trellis addition that opens a pre-filled GitHub issue using the repo URL from `siteConfig.customFields.repoUrl`

```jsx title="Simplified structure in DocItem/Content/index.js"
export default function DocItemContent({children}) {
  const syntheticTitle = useSyntheticTitle();
  return (
    <div className="markdown">
      {syntheticTitle && (
        <header>
          <Heading as="h1">{syntheticTitle}</Heading>
          {/* ← Last-updated row renders here, at the top */}
          <DocItemHeader />
        </header>
      )}
      <MDXContent>{children}</MDXContent>
    </div>
  );
}
```

## Configuration

The date and author are set per-page via frontmatter:

```yaml title="Any .mdx file"
---
last_update:
  date: 02/20/2026
  author: Your Name
---
```

The `author` field is useful as a subject matter expert reference for contributors. To control whether the author name is displayed on the page, toggle `showAuthor` in `config/site.ts`:

```ts title="config/site.ts"
lastUpdated: {
  showAuthor: false,  // true to display the author next to the date
},
```

When `showAuthor` is `false` (the default), only the date renders. The author remains in the frontmatter for contributor reference.

## The "Suggest an Edit" Link

The "Suggest an Edit" link is a Trellis feature that creates a pre-filled GitHub issue. It uses the `repoUrl` custom field:

```ts title="config/site.ts"
customFields: {
  repoUrl: 'https://github.com/your-org/your-repo',
},
```

When clicked, it opens: `{repoUrl}/issues/new?title={pageTitle}&body=Issue with: {pagePermalink}`

To disable it, remove the `repoUrl` custom field.
